title: adenolinum pallescens
side_effects: Adenolinum pallescens can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing respiratory issues, including shortness of breath and asthma-like symptoms. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can also lead to skin irritation, including redness, itching, and rashes. In severe cases, improper use of Adenolinum pallescens can cause gastrointestinal problems, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Additionally, some individuals may experience neurological symptoms, such as headaches, dizziness, and confusion, if they ingest or come into contact with the plant's toxic substances.
